title: Mail Clerk

rationales.taskAutomation: Digital mail intake and routing is increasingly automated through scan-and-distribute systems, but physical sorting, package handling, and delivery remain manual tasks.
rationales.toolMaturity: Mailroom digitization tools exist and are improving but are less universally deployed than document AI in other back-office functions.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is uneven — larger corporate campuses have digitized mail intake, but many mid-size and smaller offices still run manual mailrooms.
rationales.agenticExposure: Digital mail intake and routing is agent-ownable; physical sorting and last-mile internal delivery are not.

tasksAtRisk: Sorting and routing incoming physical mail
Digital mail scanning and inbox distribution
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 43-9051 (Mail Clerks and Mail Machine Operators, Except Postal Service), p25-p75 rounded
insight: Scan-and-distribute systems now handle a growing share of digital mail routing, but someone still has to physically sort packages and hand-deliver sensitive documents. That keeps this role at Moderate risk rather than pushing it higher.
